title: Allow dst and destination for target in secret mounts
body:
Fixes: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/3396

Patch from @mixedCase

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
It is talked about in the buildah-run man page.  But not the buildah bud man page.
It maybe should be documented in the non-existent Containerfile man page.  There is a Dockerfile man page, but we don't ship it, it comes from Docker.

Docker's documentation seems pretty inconsistent, `dst` is documented only [here](https://docs.docker.com/develop/develop-images/build_enhancements/#new-docker-build-secret-information), `target` is documented only [here](https://github.com/moby/buildkit/blob/master/frontend/dockerfile/docs/syntax.md#run---mounttypesecret) and `destination` isn't documented at all.
